title: Module 1
content:
Introduction to seakeeping, wind generated waves. Sinusoidal and
trochoidal wave theories. Wave spectrum, types of spectra. Ship motions in
regular waves, equations of motion, ship-wave encounter, strip theory.

title: Module 2
content:
Ship in a seaway, pitch and roll in irregular waves, Response Amplitude
Operator (RAO). Ship motion control, control of roll and pitch, active and
passive Stabilizers.
Derived responses, local motions, relative motions, slamming, deck
wetness, sea-sickness, added resistance, powering in waves, wave loads.

title: Module 3
content:
Introduction to manoeuvrability, the control loop, path keeping, various
types of motion stability. Basic hydrodynamics and equations of motion of
a manoeuvring body, control-fixed stability indices. Effect of hull
configuration on controls-fixed stability. Turning circle manoeuvre, heel
and speed loss during turn, zig-zag manoeuvre, spiral manoeuvre, pull-out
manoeuvre. IMO Manoeuvring Standards.

title: Module 4
content:
Experimental determination of hydrodynamic derivatives, straight line test,
rotating arm technique, Planar Motion Mechanism (PMM) technique.
Rudder geometry, rudder forces and moments, flow around rudder. Rudder
design process, rudder types, location, size, section shape, and number of
rudders, maximum rudder deflection angle. Other manoeuvring devices.
